title: Field consolidates around precision senolytics + KDM clocks + exercise as baseline geroprotector
description: Fight Aging's weekend roundup crystallizes three converging themes this week: senolytics must move to precision clearance of pathogenic subpopulations; Klemera-Doubal Method clocks (not Horvath/PhenoAge) detect short-window dietary intervention response; and physical activity is now meta-analytically validated as the largest population-scale lever on epigenetic age. Together they define the new shape of the longevity protocol stack.

title: Retro Biosciences $1.8B raise positions Alzheimer's protein-clearance pill as next major readout
description: Sam Altman-backed Retro Biosciences confirmed Friday's $1.8B valuation round, with CEO Joe Betts-LaCroix signaling no dose-limiting toxicities in the Alzheimer's autophagy-enhancer Phase 1 and initial data expected around August. The raise creates one of the largest non-reprogramming private longevity unicorns and sets up a near-term clinical catalyst for the broader autophagy-modulation thesis.

title: Rapamycin protocol design tension intensifies: New Zealand RAPA-EX-01 vs U of Arizona Phase 3
description: This week's RAPA-EX-01 (J Cachexia Sarcopenia Muscle) showing weekly rapamycin blunting exercise gains in older adults sits in direct tension with the launch of U of Arizona's $12M Coit-funded 2-year double-blind low-dose rapamycin Phase 3 and UT San Antonio's parallel NIA trial. The field is converging on the conclusion that dose, frequency, and the exercise stack matter more than initially assumed.

title: Platelets, HuR, and the inflammaging axis emerge as druggable beyond senescent cells
description: Nature Communications work this week showed platelet-specific HuR/ELAVL1 deletion reduces platelet-secreted inflammatory factors and delays systemic aging in aged mice. Combined with the Aging-US public-health editorial calling for layered geroscience interventions, platelets join senescent cells and immune cells as a third major druggable inflammaging node.

title: NG101 SCI Phase 2b imaging data confirm small molecules can produce structural CNS rescue
description: Nature Communications publication of NervGen NG101 Phase 2b spinal-cord-injury MRI data confirms that a small molecule can slow lesion volume expansion and preserve spinal cord cross-sectional area and myelination vs placebo — the first such evidence in chronic human CNS trauma. Read-throughs to age-related neurodegenerative programs are now mechanistically plausible rather than speculative.

title: Sirtuin 1 and Exercise-Induced Improvements in Metabolism
description: Cross-species review consolidates evidence that exercise raises SIRT1 mRNA, activity, and protein in muscle, brain, heart, and adipose — proposing SIRT1 as the central sensor of exercise benefits during aging.

title: NG101 and Structural Recovery After Cervical Spinal Cord Injury
description: Phase 2b SCI participants on NG101 showed faster lesion volume reduction and slower decline of spinal cord cross-sectional area and magnetization transfer saturation vs placebo — first MRI-imaging evidence of structural CNS rescue from a small molecule.

title: Short-Term Dietary Intervention Alters Physiological Profiles Relevant to Ageing
description: 4-week diet intervention in 104 adults aged 65-75 changed KDM deltaAge, with the omnivorous/high-carb group showing significant reduction vs high-fat controls — KDM clocks outperform epigenetic clocks for short windows.

title: Aging, Intestinal Mucosal Immunity, and the Gut Microbiota
description: In aged mice, intestinal aging linked to SASP marker increases, barrier dysfunction, altered T cells, reduced fecal IgA, inflammatory microbiome enrichment, and impaired M cell function.

title: Retinal Myeloid Cells and TREM2 in Age-Related Macular Degeneration
description: Review synthesizes evidence that retinal microglia and other myeloid cells, through TREM2 signaling, may influence dry and neovascular AMD — with translational considerations for targeting TREM2.

title: Cellular Senescence: From Pathogenic Mechanisms to Precision Anti-Aging Interventions
description: Argues senescent cells are heterogeneous and future senolytics should target functional pathogenicity (not just markers) to enable precision clearance.

title: Platelets, HuR, and Systemic Aging
description: Platelet-specific HuR/ELAVL1 deletion in aged mice reduced platelet-secreted pro-inflammatory factors, decreased organ infiltration, and delayed systemic aging — platelets emerge as druggable inflammaging node.

title: Immune Priming With LPS and Wound Repair in Aged Skin
description: A short pre-injury LPS pulse accelerated wound repair in aged mouse skin via improved immune cell activity, keratinocyte responsiveness, and epithelial reconstitution.

title: Older Mice May Offer New Insight Into Cancer and Aging
description: Melanoma spread peaked in middle-aged mice and was lower in young and very old animals, with gamma delta T cells implicated in the age-dependent pattern.

title: Role of Age on γδ T Cells in Metastatic Cutaneous Melanoma Progression
description: In aged-mouse melanoma models, middle-aged animals showed higher gamma delta T cell infiltration in metastatic lung/liver and greater spread, with PROS1 implicated in suppressing protective γδ T cells.

title: Physical Activity and DNA Methylation Clocks: Systematic Review and Meta-analysis
description: 44 studies and 145,465 participants: higher physical activity correlated with lower Horvath and GrimAge epigenetic age acceleration (not Hannum or PhenoAge) — the largest synthesis to date.

title: Social Organization and Longevity in Mammals
description: Re-analysis of 1,436 mammal species: group-living and pair-living species live longer than solitary species after controlling for body mass and phylogeny.

title: Retro Biosciences raises at $1.8 billion valuation
description: Sam Altman-backed Retro Biosciences confirmed new capital raise; first clinical trial is an oral pill enhancing protein-aggregate clearance in Alzheimer's. No dose-limiting toxicities seen; initial data expected around August.

title: Rapamycin and Exercise: New Study Finds Potential Performance Decline
description: RAPA-EX-01 RCT: older adults on weekly rapamycin completed ~2 fewer chair-stand reps, walked ~16 feet less, produced ~2.6 lb less grip force vs placebo; CRP rose in rapamycin arm. No biological age change.

title: Can a Drug Slow Aging? The Rapamycin Story
source: R. Ken Coit College of Pharmacy
description: U of Arizona launches 6-year Phase 3 double-blind low-dose rapamycin trial in older adults, evaluating frailty transitions and IL-6 inflammaging marker — funded by $12M Coit gift.

title: UT Health San Antonio launches rapamycin clinical trial for healthy aging
description: NIA-funded multi-phase rapamycin/everolimus study in 84 healthy adults 65-90, evaluating dosing, safety, and long-term effects of mTOR inhibition for healthspan.

title: Methylene Blue May Help Protect Hair Follicle Stem Cells from GLP-1 Stress
description: Maryland team shows methylene blue boosts HFSC proliferation via β-catenin activation, synergizes with minoxidil, and protects stem cells from GLP-1 receptor agonist-induced cell death — mechanistic counter for semaglutide/tirzepatide hair thinning.

title: Extending Healthspan Through Public Health and Longevity Medicine
description: Editorial: healthy longevity will require coordinated integration of public health, primary prevention, geroscience pharmaceuticals, and individualized longevity medicine.

title: Tozorakimab meets primary endpoint in Phase III MIRANDA COPD trial
description: AstraZeneca's IL-33 monoclonal hit primary endpoint in Phase 3 MIRANDA COPD trial, validating IL-33 blockade as a disease-modifying mechanism for a major disease of aging.

title: BioAge reports positive Phase 1 data for BGE-102
description: BioAge Labs reports positive Phase 1 results for BGE-102, NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitor program, supporting further development in cardiometabolic and inflammaging indications.

title: Pulmatrix and Eos SENOLYTIX Announce Merger Agreement
description: Pulmatrix (PULM) acquires Eos SENOLYTIX with $19M concurrent financing to advance MitoXcel mitochondrial peptide platform and lead candidate PTC-2105 for sarcopenia and age-related disease.

title: Insilico forms Longevity Board to oversee AI aging research
description: Insilico stands up dedicated Longevity Board to oversee its AI-driven aging research program, formalizing geroscience commitment alongside the $2.75B Eli Lilly deal.

title: David Sinclair on Yamanaka factor partial reprogramming approaching human eye trials
description: Sinclair interview: OSK (3-gene subset of Yamanaka factors) heading into human eye trials via Life Biosciences; lab using AI to screen molecules reversing 92-year-old skin cells to 20-year-old phenotype.

title: Mesenchymal stem cell preparations lead clinical antiaging field for 2026
description: Review: MSC preparations (Longeveron Lomecel-B, Chinese umbilical cord SCs) have three positive RCTs for skeletal muscle frailty and lead clinical antiaging development for 2026, ahead of rapamycin and resveratrol.

title: Targeting Longevity 2026: Berlin congress reframes aging as loss of system coordination
description: 2nd World Congress on Targeting Longevity (Berlin, April 8-9) consolidates emerging view that aging is progressive loss of coordination between biological systems — mitochondrial signaling, microbiota-brain axis, metabolic environments, and comparative biology.
